programs.name: KanCare Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and copays; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors with Medicare meeting Kansas-specific income/asset limits (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Part A/B assistance (~$2,800/year savings); SLMB: Part B premium (~$185/month); QI: Partial premium support.

programs.name: SNAP (Food Assistance)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries to improve nutrition. It supplements food budgets for low-income households.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (60+) with income typically under 130-200% FPL, assets under $4,500 (higher for elderly).
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household, max up to $291/individual (2026 rates vary by income).

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help pay heating/cooling bills and address energy crises. It offers one-time payments to utility providers or vendors.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors (60+), income ≤150% FPL, prioritized for elderly/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Average benefit $400-600 per household for heating; crisis aid up to $1,000.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility costs. It improves home safety and comfort for low-income residents.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (≤200% FPL), priority to elderly, disabled, and families with children.
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued at $5,000-10,000 per home, yielding 20-30% energy savings.

programs.name: Kansas Senior Health Insurance Counseling (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, supplements, and prescription drug plans. Counselors assist with enrollment, appeals, and cost-saving options.[5]
programs.who_its_for: All Kansas Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+, especially low-income seniors.
programs.benefit_value: Free service preventing over $500 million nationally in improper payments annually.

programs.name: Kansas Senior Nutrition Program (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious home-delivered and congregate meals to promote health and reduce hunger. Includes nutrition screening and education.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound or congregate seniors aged 60+, with priority for low-income and at-risk individuals.
programs.benefit_value: 1-2 meals/day, 5 days/week; suggested contribution, valued at ~$10/meal.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, counseling, and supplies to support family caregivers. Helps caregivers manage stress and continue providing care.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ or disabled adults, or grandparents raising children.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,600/year; training and counseling at low/no cost.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service jobs with training to help unemployed low-income seniors gain skills. Leads to unsubsidized employment.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors aged 55+, limited work history or dislocated workers.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$7.25-$15/hour) for 20 hours/week, plus job placement support.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Older Kansans
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for issues like benefits, housing, abuse, and consumer problems. Includes advice, representation, and education.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal needs.
programs.benefit_value: Free services worth thousands in legal fees; statewide network.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Promotes resident rights and resolves issues.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities in Kansas.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ complaints without litigation.
